note: Roman-style pinsa with impossibly light, airy dough — crispy outside, cloud-soft inside. Authentic oval shape, not round pizza. Uses rice flour blend for that signature crunch. Ranked #26 of 840 SF pizza spots. Vegan options genuinely good.

Wood-fired Neapolitan with beautifully leopard-spotted crust — crispy yet addictively chewy. Nitrate-free meats, locally sourced produce. The Diavola with lettuce on top is a clever signature. Formerly Kezar Pizzetta, rebranded but same soul.

The mozzarella bar is the real draw — bufala and burrata flown in from Italy. Margherita is balanced and light with DOP San Marzanos and Caputo flour. Some reviewers want more cheese; delivery quality can dip. Get burrata on top — it transforms the pie.

Thin-crust royalty since 2000 — cracker-crisp bottoms, chewy rims, seasonal toppings that rotate biweekly. Tiny 4-table spot with cult following. The flourless chocolate cake is a permanent must-order. Go early or wait. SF's best-kept neighborhood pizzeria.

Detroit-style squares with caramelized cheese walls and chunky tomato sauce on top. The pepperoni is a must. Pairs with Cellarmaker's rotating craft beer list. Slices available — Tuesday happy hour is the move. Deceptively small portions but deeply satisfying crust.
